site.metaTitle: AFOR — Facilities for mining and industry | Architecture and Engineering
site.metaDescription: Chilean firm specialized in architecture, engineering and project management for the development of facilities for large-scale mining and industry. Over 10 years of experience with BHP, Codelco and Tier 1 engineering firms.

hero.summary: We are a Chilean firm specialized in the development of facilities for mining and industrial operations. Over ten years integrating architecture, engineering and technical criteria in projects across the sector.

stripe.lineTwo: of facilities for mining and industry.

about.titleStart: We don't sell hours. We apply
about.p1: We develop facilities for mining and industry: offices, change houses, control rooms, electrical and technical rooms, truck shops, hazardous waste storage and warehousing areas. We integrate architecture, engineering and multidisciplinary coordination under a single technical direction.
about.p2: We embed within the project team as an extension of your engineering. We adopt the client's coding, manuals and delivery standards, and operate under their internal protocols to ensure deliverables compatible with their document management system.
about.p3: The team that takes the project from technical surveying is the same one that coordinates the final delivery. Complete technical continuity, with no internal handoffs or intermediaries.

principles.p1Body: Each facility responds to the logic of the production process: operations, personnel flow, safety and service continuity.
principles.p2Body: Mastery of regulations and safety standards for large-scale mining and industry. Complete documentation for approvals.
principles.p3Body: We work in Revit/BIM with the coding your project requires. Coordinated models, free of interferences.
principles.p4Body: Dedicated team for the project, no overhead. Aligned with your processes, manuals and schedules.

projects.subtitle: A representative selection of projects in large-scale mining and industry.

list.title: CHO Building Remediation
list.body: Comprehensive remediation of 420 m² to convert the CHO building into a high-availability technical facility. Internal layout, accesses, technical room for electrical equipment and UPS, offices, cowork areas and architectural improvements in ceilings, furniture and auxiliary systems.

list.title: Autonomous Trucks Facilities
list.body: Design of supporting facilities for the Autonomous Trucks program at Minera Escondida. Drilling Control Room with raised technical floor, wood finishes, multiscreen monitoring stations, precision climate control and regulatory signage. Interim engineering ready for construction.

list.title: GOM Control Room
list.body: Complete intervention with linear metal ceiling, wooden batten wall finish, recessed linear LED lighting and ergonomic distribution of operator stations with articulated arms and multi-screen monitors. Design that prioritizes operational concentration, acoustic comfort and luminous control for extended shifts.

list.title: Civic District and Infrastructure
list.body: Architectural design of the civic district and supporting infrastructure for the Las Yacas mining project. Definition of administrative offices, access control, restrooms and change rooms with modular distribution oriented to optimize circulation flows and regulatory compliance. Site layouts, parking and vehicle accesses integrated with the general site plan.

list.title: Industrial and Corporate Building
list.body: Architectural design of an industrial and corporate building for Mecasfy. Industrial warehouse with integrated administrative offices, translucent polycarbonate facade with exposed metallic structure, differentiated vehicle and pedestrian accesses, perimeter landscaping and parking.

list.title: Corporate Offices
list.body: Fit-out of new corporate offices for Grant Thornton Chile. Elegant and modern design with natural wood furniture, exposed ceiling with linear LED lighting, metal shelves with integrated vegetation as room dividers and collaborative workstations. Priority on natural light, warmth of materials and open distribution.

services.subtitle: Technical services integrated with the project team, under the client's protocols.

items.title: Plot Plan for facilities
items.body: Conceptual design of facilities for mining and industrial sites: layouts, architectural program, operational flows, surface estimation and master plan coordinated with structural and MEP disciplines.

items.title: Administrative offices and change houses
items.body: Design and execution of personnel spaces: technical offices, meeting rooms, dining areas, change houses for operators, locker rooms and restrooms under mining regulations.

items.title: Control, server, electrical and technical rooms
items.body: Critical rooms with raised technical floor, precision climate control, lighting and acoustic management, ergonomic distribution of operator stations with multi-screen monitors and specialized finishes.

items.title: Hazardous waste, warehousing and truck shops
items.body: Technical construction for hazardous waste, industrial warehouses and heavy equipment maintenance shops, with regulatory compliance and certified operational safety.

items.title: Multidisciplinary coordination and counterpart review
items.body: Integration of structural, mechanical, electrical, instrumentation and civil disciplines. Technical review of third-party plans and documents under the client's standards.

items.title: Site supervision and progress
items.body: Technical inspection on site, control of physical progress, validation of deliverables, management of non-conformities and project closure with as-built documentation.

items.title: Multidisciplinary Project Management
items.body: Comprehensive project management: planning, deadline and cost control, supplier coordination, risk management and executive reporting to the client.

contact.body: Tell us what you need. We respond within 24 business hours.
